title: No-bake chocolate cheesecake
subtitle: Fridge-set cheesecake with dark chocolate filling
excerpt: A fridge-set cheesecake with a digestive biscuit base and a dark chocolate cream cheese filling. Melted chocolate folded into whipped cream cheese gives the filling a clean, fudge-like texture. No oven, no gelatine.
sourceNotes: The no-bake cheesecake is a British domestic formula of the mid-twentieth century. The chocolate version uses melted dark chocolate in place of a fruit-flavoured element. The method (whipped double cream folded into cream cheese for stability without gelatine) is the same as for any no-bake cheesecake.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the night before and refrigerate overnight. Unmould up to 1 hour before serving.

glossaryTerms.definition: Melted chocolate must be allowed to cool to near room temperature before folding into cream cheese or whipped cream. Added too warm, the heat melts the fat in the dairy and the filling becomes liquid. Added too cold, the chocolate seizes into lumps. The chocolate should feel warm but not hot — around 30–35°C.

content.text: No-bake cheesecake works on one principle: whipped double cream folded into cream cheese gives the filling enough structure to hold its shape in the fridge, without gelatine. The chocolate goes in as melted dark chocolate, cooled to near room temperature before it is added.
The temperature of the melted chocolate matters.
to near room temperature before adding it to the cream cheese is the step that most people skip and most people regret.
Crush the {{digestive-biscuits}} to fine crumbs. Mix with the melted {{unsalted-butter}} until evenly dampened. Press into the base of the springform tin. Refrigerate while you make the filling.

Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and leave to cool for 15–20 minutes until it feels warm but not hot to the touch.

Beat the {{full-fat-cream-cheese}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} together until smooth. Stir in the cooled chocolate until combined. In a separate bowl, whip the {{double-cream}} to soft peaks. Fold the whipped cream into the chocolate cream cheese in three additions.

Spoon onto the biscuit base and smooth the top. Refrigerate for at least 6 hours, overnight if possible. To unmould, run a warm knife around the inside of the tin before unclipping. Dust the top with {{cocoa-powder}} before serving.
